proposed.name: dag-structural-types
proposed.claim: DAG node type field accepts exactly four values - primitive, compound, inference, directive - one per epistemic operation (attest, aggregate, infer, prescribe) - and determines which other fields are meaningful

rules.given: A node is being written to the DAG
rules.then: The type must be one of: primitive, compound, inference, directive
The node must have a non-null artifact field

rules.given: A node has type: compound or type: inference
rules.then: The node must have a non-empty deps array

rules.given: A node has rules or invariants populated
rules.then: The node must have type: directive (descriptive types cannot carry contracts)

invariants: Every active node has exactly one type
type is a closed enum: primitive | compound | inference | directive
Only directives can carry rules or invariants
Structural type determines valid operations (only directives are materialized)

reasoning: cb-schema-v2 D1-D6 (plans/cb-schema-v2/design.md): the type enum opens to four values, one per epistemic operation; rules and materialization re-attach to directives. The stale v1 'source' field reference is corrected to 'artifact' in passing.
